data.description:
A mail hauberk in the listed style covers not only the wearer’s chest, but also their arms, with a long skirt extended to the knee. As with a mail shirt, the padding beneath the armor is sometimes so thick as to qualify as protective gear in its own right

Heavy armor is the thickest, toughest panoply available on the market, and its bulk and noise make its encumbrance apply as a penalty to Sneak or Exert checks made by the wearer.
